Name: BeaconHaus Companion
Description: Rulebook (single source of truth) for the BeaconHaus Companion. Hand-authored from README.md, beaconhaus_schema.sql, and BeaconHaus_Companion_Architecture.md. The Supabase tables, RLS policies, controlled vocabularies, and frontend config are derivative outputs generated from this hub. Change meaning here, then run effortless build.

Profiles.Description: Per-user settings, one row per user. Holds the gentle parameters that drive the daily ritual. This is the ownership root: every owned row points back here through its Owner relationship.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical key for the profile, a slug of the account key. Maps to the per-user identity in the substrate.

schema.name: AccountKey
schema.Description: Stable per-user identity key. Maps to user_id in the generated schema. Owned rows reference this value through their Owner relationship.

schema.name: DisplayName
schema.Description: Friendly display name shown in the app. Default Liz. Maps to profile.display_name.

schema.name: Tagline
schema.Description: Personal tagline. Default Illuminating Life, Legacy, and Love. Maps to profile.tagline.

schema.name: WeeklyWarmupGoal
schema.Description: Target number of hellos per week, shown as encouragement, never a backlog. Default 4. Maps to profile.weekly_warmup_goal. See GovernanceRules R4.

schema.name: WarmThresholdDays
schema.Description: A person stays Warm within this many days of the last hello. Default 14. Maps to profile.warm_threshold_days. See GovernanceRules R2.

schema.name: CoolingThresholdDays
schema.Description: A person is Cooling up to this many days since the last hello, then Cold beyond it. Default 30. Maps to profile.cooling_threshold_days. See GovernanceRules R2.

schema.name: MorningWarmupCount
schema.Description: How many people the Today screen surfaces each morning, one at a time. Default 3. Maps to profile.morning_warmup_count. See GovernanceRules R3.

schema.name: WeekStart
schema.Description: Anchor for the weekly count, set to the most recent Monday. Maps to profile.week_start. See GovernanceRules R4.

schema.name: WeekCount
schema.Description: Hellos logged since WeekStart, maintained by the app and reset each Monday. Default 0. Maps to profile.week_count.

schema.name: People
schema.Description: The people owned by this profile.

schema.name: Facilities
schema.Description: The facilities owned by this profile.

schema.name: Captures
schema.Description: The captures owned by this profile.

schema.name: OAuthTokens
schema.Description: The server-side OAuth tokens owned by this profile.

schema.name: CountOfPeople
schema.Description: Number of people in this profile's warm network.

schema.name: CountOfFacilities
schema.Description: Number of target facilities owned by this profile.

schema.name: CountOfCaptures
schema.Description: Number of captures owned by this profile.

People.Description: The warm network. Liz's sphere of influence and community ecosystem, anyone who can open a door or help map who is aligned.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical key for the person, a slug of FullName.

schema.name: FullName
schema.Description: The person's name. The only required field on a person. Maps to people.name. See GovernanceRules R6.

schema.name: Owner
schema.Description: The profile that owns this row. Holds the Profiles Name. Drives Row Level Security. Maps to people.user_id. See GovernanceRules R1.

schema.name: RoleOrg
schema.Description: The person's role or organization. Maps to people.role_org.

schema.name: Sphere
schema.Description: Which sphere of the network this person belongs to. Holds the Spheres Name. Maps to people.sphere.

schema.name: Relationship
schema.Description: How Liz knows this person, in her own words. Maps to people.relationship.

schema.name: DoorsCanOpen
schema.Description: What doors this person can open or who they can introduce. Maps to people.doors_can_open.

schema.name: TheAsk
schema.Description: The specific ask or next favor, kept human and light. Maps to people.the_ask.

schema.name: Email
schema.Description: Email address, used by the native email compose link. Maps to people.email.

schema.name: Phone
schema.Description: Phone number. Maps to people.phone.

schema.name: LinkedinUrl
schema.Description: LinkedIn profile URL, used only for Open in LinkedIn deep links. Maps to people.linkedin_url. See GovernanceRules R10.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Where this relationship stands. Holds the PersonStatuses Name. Defaults to to-reach-out. Maps to people.status.

schema.name: LastTouched
schema.Description: Date of the most recent hello. Logging a hello sets this to today. Drives Warmth. Maps to people.last_touched. See GovernanceRules R2 and R3.

schema.name: NextStep
schema.Description: The gentle next step with this person. Maps to people.next_step.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Free notes. Maps to people.notes.

schema.name: WarmThresholdDays
schema.Description: The owner's warm threshold in days, pulled from the owning profile. Falls back to 14 when the profile leaves it blank, so a missing threshold never silently turns every contact cold.

schema.name: CoolingThresholdDays
schema.Description: The owner's cooling threshold in days, pulled from the owning profile. Falls back to 30 when the profile leaves it blank.

schema.name: DaysSinceLastTouched
schema.Description: Days since the last hello, or 0 when never touched. Time relative, resolved at view-compute time. ISBLANK guards the unset (NULL) date.

schema.name: IsNeverTouched
schema.Description: True when there is no recorded hello yet. A never touched contact reads as ready for a hello, never as overdue. See GovernanceRules R5.

schema.name: Warmth
schema.Description: Derived warmth: warm within WarmThresholdDays, cooling up to CoolingThresholdDays, otherwise cold, including never touched. Matches a WarmthLevels Name. Drives sorting and the daily ritual only. See GovernanceRules R2.

Facilities.Description: The target places. Values aligned independent living and 55 and over communities first, the places Liz is working toward.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical key for the facility, a slug of FacilityName.

schema.name: FacilityName
schema.Description: The facility name. The only required field on a facility. Maps to facilities.facility_name. See GovernanceRules R6.

schema.name: Owner
schema.Description: The profile that owns this row. Holds the Profiles Name. Drives Row Level Security. Maps to facilities.user_id. See GovernanceRules R1.

schema.name: Type
schema.Description: The kind of facility. Holds the FacilityTypes Name. Maps to facilities.type.

schema.name: Town
schema.Description: Town where the facility is located. Maps to facilities.town.

schema.name: Region
schema.Description: Region grouping for the facility. Holds the Regions Name. Maps to facilities.region.

schema.name: LeadRoute
schema.Description: How Liz can reach this facility, warm or cold. Holds the LeadRoutes Name. Maps to facilities.lead_route.

schema.name: Aligned
schema.Description: Whether the facility seems values aligned. Holds the AlignmentLevels Name. Maps to facilities.aligned.

schema.name: DecisionMaker
schema.Description: Name of the person who decides. Maps to facilities.decision_maker.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Title of the decision maker. Maps to facilities.title.

schema.name: Email
schema.Description: Contact email. Maps to facilities.email.

schema.name: Phone
schema.Description: Contact phone. Maps to facilities.phone.

schema.name: Website
schema.Description: Facility website. Maps to facilities.website.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Where the outreach stands. Holds the FacilityStatuses Name. Defaults to to-research. Maps to facilities.status.

schema.name: NextStep
schema.Description: The gentle next step with this facility. Maps to facilities.next_step.

schema.name: FitNotes
schema.Description: Notes on fit and alignment. Maps to facilities.fit_notes.

schema.name: RegionLabel
schema.Description: Human readable region label, pulled from the related region.

schema.name: LeadRouteLabel
schema.Description: Human readable lead route label, pulled from the related lead route.

schema.name: IsWarmLead
schema.Description: True when there is a warm route into this facility rather than a cold approach.

data.NextStep: Research the community and confirm the right contact

Captures.Description: Pain points, case studies, and ideas, captured as they come. Feeds the marketing package.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical key for the capture, a slug of Title.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Short title of the capture. The only required field on a capture. Maps to captures.title. See GovernanceRules R6.

schema.name: Owner
schema.Description: The profile that owns this row. Holds the Profiles Name. Drives Row Level Security. Maps to captures.user_id. See GovernanceRules R1.

schema.name: Type
schema.Description: The kind of capture. Holds the CaptureTypes Name. Defaults to idea. Maps to captures.type.

schema.name: Detail
schema.Description: The full text of the capture. Maps to captures.detail.

data.Title: Families feel overwhelmed by later-life planning
data.Detail: Families often do not know where to begin with later-life planning and feel alone in it. A calm guide reduces the overwhelm.

data.Title: Facilities want a warmer guide for residents and families
data.Detail: Communities want a gentle, human way to support residents and families through later-life planning and connection, led by community first rather than by clinical language.

data.Title: A Warwick family found calm through guided planning
data.Detail: A local family moved from anxiety to calm by walking through their later-life plan with a trusted guide, one small step at a time.

data.Title: Monthly community gathering on living fully in later life
data.Detail: Host a warm monthly gathering at a partner facility focused on living fully in later life, legacy, and connection.

OAuthTokens.Description: Server-side storage for Drive, LinkedIn, and optional Calendar and Gmail tokens. Never exposed to the client. Read and refreshed only in server code. See GovernanceRules R12.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical key for the token, a compound of owner and provider.

schema.name: Owner
schema.Description: The profile that owns this token. Holds the Profiles Name. Maps to oauth_tokens.user_id. See GovernanceRules R1.

schema.name: Provider
schema.Description: The integration this token is for, for example google-drive, linkedin, google-calendar, or gmail. Maps to oauth_tokens.provider.

schema.name: AccessToken
schema.Description: Short lived access token. Server-side only, never sent to the client. Maps to oauth_tokens.access_token. See GovernanceRules R12.

schema.name: RefreshToken
schema.Description: Long lived refresh token, stored encrypted and tied to the owner. Server-side only. Maps to oauth_tokens.refresh_token. See GovernanceRules R12.

schema.name: Scope
schema.Description: The granted OAuth scope, kept as narrow as the feature needs. Maps to oauth_tokens.scope. See GovernanceRules R11.

schema.name: ExpiresAt
schema.Description: When the access token expires. Maps to oauth_tokens.expires_at.

Spheres.Description: Controlled vocabulary for people.sphere. The part of the network a person belongs to. Generated as a derivative enum table. Do not hardcode these in the frontend or schema.

WarmthLevels.Description: Controlled vocabulary for the derived warmth value. Warmth is computed from LastTouched and is not stored. These rows give the frontend the labels and gentle framing for each level. See GovernanceRules R2 and R5.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Slug key for the warmth level, derived from the label. Matches the value returned by People.Warmth.

schema.name: WarmthLabel
schema.Description: Human readable warmth label shown in the app.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What this warmth level means.

schema.name: Guidance
schema.Description: Gentle framing shown to Liz. No guilt, a cold contact reads as ready for a hello. See GovernanceRules R5.

schema.name: SortOrder
schema.Description: Display order, warmest first.

data.Description: Between the warm and cooling thresholds.
data.Guidance: A good time for a gentle hello.

data.Description: Beyond the cooling threshold or never touched.

GovernanceRules.Description: The governance rules for the BeaconHaus Companion, one row per rule. Each rule is meaning that lives in the rulebook and is traceable. The frontend and backend enforce these because they are generated from them, not because someone wrote them into code by hand.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Slug key for the rule, derived from the title.

schema.name: RuleCode
schema.Description: Short rule code, R1 through R12.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Short title of the rule.

schema.name: Statement
schema.Description: The full rule statement, the meaning that downstream artifacts must honor.

schema.name: Category
schema.Description: Grouping for the rule, for example Ownership, Warmth, Ritual, Content, Positioning, Integration, Security, or UX.

schema.name: EnforcedBy
schema.Description: Where this rule is realized in the derivative outputs.

schema.name: SortOrder
schema.Description: Display order, R1 through R12.

data.Title: Ownership
data.Statement: Every row belongs to one user through the Owner relationship to Profiles. Row Level Security limits all access to that user. Realized in the generated RLS policies.
data.EnforcedBy: Generated RLS policies, keyed off the Owner relationship

data.Title: Warmth Model
data.Statement: Warmth is derived from LastTouched. A person is Warm within WarmThresholdDays, Cooling up to CoolingThresholdDays, and Cold beyond that or when never touched. Warmth drives sorting and the daily ritual only.
data.EnforcedBy: People.Warmth calculated field and the threshold lookups

data.Title: Daily Ritual
data.Statement: The Today screen surfaces MorningWarmupCount people, coldest or never touched first, one at a time. Logging a hello sets LastTouched to today and counts toward the weekly total.

data.Title: Weekly Count
data.Statement: The weekly count is counted since the most recent Monday and resets each Monday. It is shown as encouragement toward WeeklyWarmupGoal, never as a backlog.
data.EnforcedBy: App weekly counter, Profiles.WeekStart and Profiles.WeekCount

data.Title: No Guilt
data.Statement: No overdue, late, or warning states anywhere. A cold contact reads as ready for a hello. Empty states are warm and inviting.

data.Title: Minimal Friction
data.Statement: Only FullName for a person or FacilityName for a facility is required to create a row. Every other field is optional.
data.EnforcedBy: Field nullability, only the Name source fields are required

data.Title: Status Auto Advance
data.Statement: Logging a first hello moves a person from To reach out to Reached out. Further status changes are manual.
data.EnforcedBy: App mutation on the first logged hello

data.Title: Content Punctuation
data.Statement: Never use em dashes or en dashes in any label, message, or generated text. Use hyphens only inside hyphenated words. This is a BeaconHaus content rule.

data.Title: Positioning
data.Statement: Do not lead with death literacy in the interface. Lead with later-life planning and community connection.

data.Title: LinkedIn Safety
data.Statement: No scraping or browser automation of LinkedIn, ever. The official API cannot read connections, search people, or send requests or messages for this app, and unofficial tools risk her account. LinkedIn is limited to official posting through the member social scope and Open in LinkedIn deep links from stored profile URLs.
data.EnforcedBy: Integration layer, posting and deep links only

data.Title: Least Privilege
data.Statement: Request the narrowest OAuth scope that does the job: drive.file for Drive, calendar.events for Calendar, member social for LinkedIn posting, and Gmail send only if added later. Ask for each scope only when the feature is first used.
data.EnforcedBy: OAuth consent flow with incremental scopes

data.Title: Secrets Server Side
data.Statement: OAuth client secrets and token exchanges live only in server code. Tokens are stored in OAuthTokens, never in the client bundle.

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Hand-authored rulebook (single source of truth) for the BeaconHaus Companion. Models five entity tables (People, Facilities, Captures, Profiles, OAuthTokens), nine controlled-vocabulary enum tables (Spheres, PersonStatuses, FacilityTypes, Regions, LeadRoutes, AlignmentLevels, FacilityStatuses, CaptureTypes, WarmthLevels), and a GovernanceRules table holding R1 through R12. The Supabase schema in beaconhaus_schema.sql, its RLS policies, the controlled vocabularies, and the frontend config are derivative outputs generated from this hub. Convention: Name is the first field and is a calculated slug, there are no surrogate Id fields in the hub, foreign keys are singular relationship fields that hold the parent Name, and reverse relationships are plural.

_conversion_metadata.source: hand-authored from README.md, beaconhaus_schema.sql, and BeaconHaus_Companion_Architecture.md
_conversion_metadata.notes: created_at and updated_at are substrate audit columns maintained by triggers in the generated schema and are intentionally not modeled in the hub. Warmth is time relative and resolved at view-compute time, which requires a TODAY() style function in the rulebook-to-postgres dialect. Ownership (R1) is modeled via the Owner relationship to Profiles, from which the transpiler generates RLS keyed to the current user.
